answer:
\boxed{A}  

The study found that nude mice (T-cell deficient) exposed to 90 ppm toluene showed longer escape latency compared to wild-type mice, indicating T-cell deficiency affected spatial learning. Additionally, toluene impaired retention memory in wild-type but not in nude mice, further suggesting T cells play a role in the response. Thus, T-cell deficiency does influence spatial learning ability after toluene exposure.
